meta.title: CAPIZ
meta.subtitle: Capiz, Western Visayas
meta.description: Capiz, known as the Seafood Capital of the Philippines, is a province of Panay Island blessed with abundant marine resources, mystical folklore, and the charming city of Roxas. Its rivers, bays, and coastlines provide the freshest oysters, scallops, and crabs in the country.

attractions.name: Roxas City Plaza
attractions.description: The heart of Roxas City featuring a beautiful park, historic church, and the iconic centennial tree that has stood for centuries.

attractions.name: Capiz Shell Museum
attractions.description: A museum dedicated to the famous Capiz shell, used worldwide in lampshades and decor, showcasing its harvest and processing.
attractions.things_to_do: Museum tour, shell crafts, souvenir shopping

attractions.name: Olotayan Island
attractions.description: A pristine island off the coast of Capiz with white sand beaches, clear waters, and excellent snorkeling and diving spots.

attractions.name: Baybay Beach
attractions.description: A popular public beach in Roxas City perfect for swimming, beach strolls, and enjoying stunning sunsets over the Sibuyan Sea.

attractions.name: Panay Church
attractions.description: One of the largest churches in Asia, the Saint John the Baptist Parish in Panay is a stunning 18th century Baroque structure.

attractions.name: Sapian Oyster Farm
attractions.description: Visit one of the freshest oyster farms in the Philippines and learn the traditional harvesting process before enjoying them fresh.
attractions.things_to_do: Farm tour, oyster tasting, cultural learning

attractions.name: Capiz Zoo & Botanical Garden
attractions.description: A family-friendly zoo and garden featuring local wildlife, exotic plants, and well-maintained grounds in Roxas City.
attractions.things_to_do: Wildlife viewing, garden walk, family fun

attractions.name: Evelio B. Javier Foundation Museum
attractions.description: A museum honoring Capiz's beloved governor and human rights advocate Evelio Javier, featuring historical exhibits.

attractions.name: Lawaan Spring
attractions.description: A natural freshwater spring surrounded by lush vegetation, perfect for a refreshing dip and nature immersion.

activities.name: Seafood Boodle Fight
activities.description: Experience a traditional Filipino communal feast with the freshest Capiz seafood — oysters, crabs, shrimps — served on banana leaves.
activities.things_to_do: Seafood feast, cultural dining, group bonding

activities.name: Island Hopping
activities.description: Explore the small islands off Capiz's coast by boat, visiting pristine beaches and snorkeling spots teeming with marine life.

activities.name: Capiz River Tour
activities.description: A scenic boat tour along the Capiz River through mangrove forests and fishing communities, learning about the local way of life.
activities.things_to_do: Boat tour, mangrove walk, bird watching

activities.name: Traditional Fishing
activities.description: Join local fishermen for an early morning fishing trip in the bountiful waters of Capiz Bay using traditional methods.

activities.name: Scallop Farm Visit
activities.description: Tour a scallop aquaculture farm in Capiz to see how these prized shellfish are cultivated before being harvested.
activities.things_to_do: Farm tour, scallop tasting, cultural learning

activities.name: Heritage Biking Tour
activities.description: Pedal through Roxas City's heritage sites, colonial churches, and riverside paths on a guided bicycle tour.

activities.name: Capiz Shell Craft Workshop
activities.description: Learn to create beautiful lampshades and decor items from the famous Capiz shell in a hands-on workshop.

activities.name: Mangrove Planting
activities.description: Participate in a mangrove reforestation activity in Capiz's coastal areas and contribute to the environment.

activities.name: Sunrise at Baybay Beach
activities.description: Wake up early and watch the stunning sunrise over the Sibuyan Sea from Baybay Beach in Roxas City.

food.name: Fresh Capiz Oysters
food.description: The freshest oysters in the Philippines, harvested from Capiz Bay and served raw or grilled with calamansi and chili.

food.name: Scallop Dishes
food.description: Capiz scallops are among the largest and sweetest in Asia, served baked, grilled, or in rich butter and garlic sauce.

food.name: Baye-Baye
food.description: Capiz's famous native delicacy made from toasted young glutinous rice, coconut milk, and sugar — a sweet sticky treat.

food.name: Piyaya
food.description: A flat, unleavened bread filled with muscovado sugar — Iloilo and Capiz's most popular pasalubong (souvenir food gift).

food.name: Crab & Curacha
food.description: Fresh mud crabs and curacha (spanner crab) from Capiz Bay prepared in various styles — steamed, in coconut milk, or sweet and spicy.

food.name: Camalig Restaurant
food.description: The most famous seafood restaurant in Capiz, serving the freshest local catch in a charming riverside setting.

food.name: Tinuom na Manok
food.description: Chicken stuffed with lemongrass and ginger, wrapped in banana leaves and slow-cooked — a traditional Capizeño recipe.

food.name: Seafood Kare-kare
food.description: A Capiz twist on the classic Filipino peanut stew, using fresh prawns and crab instead of oxtail for a coastal variation.

food.name: Capiz Halo-halo
food.description: A refreshing Capiz version of the classic Filipino shaved ice dessert, featuring local fruits and the region's unique coconut delicacies.

events.name: Sinadya sa Halaran
events.description: Capiz's grandest festival held every Christmas season, a colorful celebration of thanksgiving for the bountiful harvest of the sea.
events.things_to_do: Street dancing, cultural shows, food fair

events.name: Capiz Seafood Festival
events.description: An annual showcase of Capiz's seafood wealth featuring cooking contests, seafood markets, and celebrity chef demonstrations.
events.things_to_do: Food tasting, cooking shows, seafood market

events.name: Manggahan Festival
events.description: A festival celebrating the mango harvest season in Capiz, with trade fairs, cultural presentations, and mango-based products.
events.things_to_do: Mango tasting, trade fair, cultural shows

events.name: Capiz Foundation Day
events.description: The anniversary of Capiz province, celebrated with parades, cultural programs, and events highlighting provincial heritage.

events.name: Ati-Atihan Capiz
events.description: Capiz joins the Western Visayas in celebrating the Ati-Atihan tradition, honoring the Ati people with tribal dances and costumes.

events.name: Fiesta Roxas City
events.description: Roxas City's founding anniversary celebration with street parties, beauty pageants, sports competitions, and nightly entertainment.

events.name: International Fishing Tournament
events.description: An annual fishing competition drawing anglers from across the Philippines and abroad to the rich waters of Capiz Bay.

events.name: Capiz Shell Craft Fair
events.description: An annual fair showcasing and selling handcrafted products made from Capiz shells — lampshades, wind chimes, and decor.

events.name: Traslacion de Nuestra Señora
events.description: A solemn religious procession honoring Capiz's patron saints, featuring antique religious images carried through heritage streets.

accommodations.name: Roxas President Hotel
accommodations.description: The premier hotel in Roxas City offering comfortable rooms, a swimming pool, and easy access to the city's seafood restaurants.

accommodations.name: Hotel Rafael
accommodations.description: A centrally located hotel in Roxas City with modern amenities, friendly staff, and proximity to local attractions.

accommodations.name: Capiz Beach Resort
accommodations.description: A beachfront resort on the shores of Baybay Beach offering cottages, a pool, and stunning ocean views.

accommodations.name: Halaran Plaza Hotel
accommodations.description: A comfortable hotel overlooking the city plaza, ideal for travelers wanting to be in the heart of Roxas City.

accommodations.name: Avenue Pension House
accommodations.description: A budget-friendly pension house offering clean, comfortable rooms in the center of Roxas City.

accommodations.name: Olotayan Island Resort
accommodations.description: An island escape resort on Olotayan Island accessible by boat from Roxas City, perfect for a secluded beach getaway.

accommodations.name: Casa Ceres Hotel
accommodations.description: A charming heritage-inspired hotel with colonial decor, a garden, and a restaurant serving authentic Capizeño cuisine.

accommodations.name: Camia Resort & Restaurant
accommodations.description: A relaxing resort with pool, nipa cottages, and a popular restaurant known for fresh seafood dishes.

accommodations.name: Capiz Inn
accommodations.description: A no-frills budget inn offering clean rooms and basic amenities for budget-conscious travelers exploring Capiz.
